source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/06_(Retrofit) HnX-R1/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/11_Clinical Evaluation/Clinical Image Report/010_Clinical Image Report  - A1417MCW.docx

document.title: Clinical Considerations 2
document.documentType: Clinical Image Report / clinical evaluation attachment

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical image comparison report for A1417MCW against HAD1417MCW using plain radiographic images and DICOM 3.0 image acquisition.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation by documenting image quality, exposure conditions, expert qualifications, and comparison conclusions for regulatory submission context referencing FDA expectations.
keyPoints: Objective Device: A1417MCW by H&abyz; Comparative Equipment: HAD1417MCW made by H&Abyz.
Prepared, reviewed, and approved on Dec/22/2022 by R&D and Quality personnel.
The report compares 30 radiographic views and lists total average image annotation scores of 3.62 for HAD1417MCW and 3.63 for A1417MCW.
The conclusion states A1417MCW images are comparable or superior to the same view from a similar patient with HAD1417MCW.

Attachment justification states Professor Sung Hyun Kim, M.D., Ph.D. is a board-certified radiologist in Korea with over 30 years of experience and equivalent qualifications to a U.S. board-certified radiologist.

evidence.claim: The document identifies A1417MCW as the model and objective device.
evidence.evidenceText: Model: A1417MCW Objective Device : A1417MCW by H&abyz (On the Right)

evidence.claim: The comparative equipment is HAD1417MCW made by H&Abyz.
evidence.evidenceText: Comparative Equipment : HAD1417MCW made by H&Abyz (On the Left)

evidence.claim: The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on Dec/22/2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Kim, Kihyun Dec/22/2022 Review R&D Manager Lee Dongjin Dec/22/2022 Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Dec/22/2022

evidence.claim: The document uses DICOM 3.0 for image acquisition and output data formatting.
evidence.evidenceText: By these presents output data for communications standard DICOM 3.0 is used to format at the data for downloading and analysis

evidence.claim: The report compares 30 annotation average values and reports total averages of 3.62 for HAD1417MCW and 3.63 for A1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Average Value of each annotation ... Total Average 3.62 3.63

evidence.claim: The clinical conclusion states A1417MCW images are comparable or superior to HAD1417MCW images for the same view from a similar patient.
evidence.evidenceText: After comparing a broad review of plain radiographic images taken with the A1417MCW and the HAD1417MCW, the images obtained with the A1417MCW are comparable or superior to the same view obtained from a similar patient with the HAD1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The conclusion states spatial and soft tissue contrast resolution are superior using A1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: In general, both the spatial and soft tissue contrast resolution are superior using the A1417MCW. Specifically, the soft tissues on extremity films were seen with better clarity.
evidence.pageTarget: Spatial and Soft Tissue Contrast Resolution

evidence.claim: The conclusion states there is no difficulty evaluating a wide range of anatomic structures needed to provide a correct conclusion.
evidence.evidenceText: There is no difficulty in evaluating a wide range of anatomic structures necessary to provide a correct conclusion.

evidence.claim: Professor Sung Hyun Kim is identified as the selected clinical evaluation expert and a Korean board-certified radiologist with over 30 years of experience.
evidence.evidenceText: The selected clinical evaluation expert, Professor Sung Hyun Kim, M.D., Ph.D., is a board-certified radiologist in Korea with over 30 years of clinical, academic, and research experience in diagnostic and interventional radiology.

evidence.claim: The expert justification states the FDA requirement is evaluation by a U.S. radiologist or equivalently qualified expert.
evidence.evidenceText: fulfilling the requirement set forth by the FDA that the evaluation be conducted by a U.S. radiologist or an equivalently qualified expert

evidence.claim: The expert justification states Dr. Kim's qualifications satisfy the FDA requirement for a radiologist of U.S.-equivalent standing.
evidence.evidenceText: His experience and qualifications demonstrate a level of expertise comparable to internationally recognized credentialing standards, and thus fully satisfy the FDA's requirement for evaluation by a radiologist of U.S.-equivalent standing.

evidence.claim: Knee AP section contains a model reference to F1417MCW instead of A1417MCW.

reviewFlags.reason: No revision or version number is present in the provided source text.
Authority is ambiguous: FDA is referenced as setting an expert qualification requirement, but the document itself appears to be an internal clinical image report rather than an FDA-issued document.
Knee AP section lists F1417MCW while most of the document identifies the objective device as A1417MCW; this may be conflicting evidence or a transcription error.
OCR/text quality issues are present, including apparent typos such as DCIOM 3.0, Sternal Angel, Insperction, and Korean Imagin Society.
